title: CCPA/CPRA — Sensitive PI — Precise Geolocation Collection Stop Signal After User Opt-Out

compliance.assurance_rationale: CCPA/CPRA §1798.121 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA/CPRA §1798.121 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.4.9: CCPA/CPRA — Sensitive PI — Precise Geolocation Collection Stop Signal After User Opt-Out.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.4.9' running on sourcetype consent:state and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), mobile analytics HEC
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="consent:state" geo_collection_allowed=false earliest=-30d
| eval opt=strptime(updated_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left profile_id [
    search index=app sourcetype="mobile:location_event" earliest=-30d
    | where accuracy_m<=50
    | fields profile_id, _time, accuracy_m
  ]
| where _time>opt
| stats count by profile_id, accuracy_m
| sort - count
description: Sensitive personal information includes precise geolocation. Detecting mobile or web telemetry that continues after an opt-out timestamp demonstrates downstream systems honor consumer choices and reduces enforcement exposure under §1798.121.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app, privacy and sourcetypes consent:state, mobile:location_event. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align `accuracy_m` threshold with your “precise” definition; (2) hash `profile_id` in shared dashboards if required; (3) route to privacy engineering for pipeline fixes; (4) document lawful exceptions (e.g., emergency services) in a lookup; (5) retain evidence per records schedule. Indexes required: `index=app`, `index=privacy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=consent:state`, `sourcetype=mobile:location_event`.
visualization: Table (violations), Timeline (`_time` vs opt), Single value (affected profiles).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), mobile analytics HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (profile_id, geo_collection_allowed, updated_ts); `index=app` `sourcetype="mobile:location_event"` (profile_id, lat, lon, accuracy_m, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align `accuracy_m` threshold with your “precise” definition; (2) hash `profile_id` in shared dashboards if required; (3) route to privacy engineering for pipeline fixes; (4) document lawful exceptions (e.g., emergency services) in a lookup; (5) retain evidence per records schedule.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="consent:state" geo_collection_allowed=false earliest=-30d
| eval opt=strptime(updated_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left profile_id [
    search index=app sourcetype="mobile:location_event" earliest=-30d
    | where accuracy_m<=50
    | fields profile_id, _time, accuracy_m
  ]
| where _time>opt
| stats count by profile_id, accuracy_m
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Sensitive PI — Precise Geolocation Collection Stop Signal After User Opt-Out** — Sensitive personal information includes precise geolocation. Detecting mobile or web telemetry that continues after an opt-out timestamp demonstrates downstream systems honor consumer choices and reduces enforcement exposure under §1798.121.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (profile_id, geo_collection_allowed, updated_ts); `index=app` `sourcetype="mobile:location_event"` (profile_id, lat, lon, accuracy_m, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), mobile analytics HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: consent:state. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="consent:state", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opt** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where _time>opt` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **profile_id, accuracy_m**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: accuracy_m, opt, profile_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (violations), Timeline (`_time` vs opt), Single value (affected profiles).
